I love this project very much but got a trouble on entering input to my program.

issue_title: NameError when providing unavailable config module
issue_body:
Tested on Python `3.5.1`
When I specify not-existing module name as --config option or in CONFIG kw, I get the following output when starting app:
```
[2016-03-16 05:53:03 +0300] [9406] [INFO] Starting gunicorn 19.4.5
[2016-03-16 05:53:03 +0300] [9406] [INFO] Listening at: http://0.0.0.0:5002 (9406)
[2016-03-16 05:53:03 +0300] [9406] [INFO] Using worker: muffin.worker.GunicornWorker
[2016-03-16 05:53:03 +0300] [9449] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 9449
ERROR:asyncio:Error in start callback
application: <Application: test>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/tmp/muffin/muffin/app.py", line 188, in start
    res = cb(self, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/tmp/muffin/muffin/app.py", line 131, in <lambda>
    lambda app: app.logger.error("Error importing %s: %s", module, exc))
NameError: free variable 'exc' referenced before assignment in enclosing scope
```
After that error application is served.

I found that `exc` variable to which exception is assigned becomes unavailable when leaving `except:` clause, even for nested functions. When I attach that same exception to another (temporary) variable, everything works fine:
```
diff --git a/muffin/app.py b/muffin/app.py
index b966f0c..c106f1b 100644
--- a/muffin/app.py
+++ b/muffin/app.py
@@ -127,8 +127,9 @@ class Application(web.Application):

             except ImportError as exc:
                 config.CONFIG = None
+                exc_save = exc  # or else it will be unavailable on Python 3.5
                 self.register_on_start(
-                    lambda app: app.logger.error("Error importing %s: %s", module, exc))
+                    lambda app: app.logger.error("Error importing %s: %s", module, exc_save))

         return config
```
results in this output:
```
[2016-03-16 05:54:25 +0300] [11874] [INFO] Starting gunicorn 19.4.5
[2016-03-16 05:54:25 +0300] [11874] [INFO] Listening at: http://0.0.0.0:5002 (11874)
[2016-03-16 05:54:25 +0300] [11874] [INFO] Using worker: muffin.worker.GunicornWorker
[2016-03-16 05:54:25 +0300] [11882] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 11882
[2016-03-16 05:54:25 +0300] [11882] [ERROR] Error importing app_cfg: No module named 'app_cfg'
```

For me that error message looks very strange, because I coultn't find any references to `exc` variable in any of enclosing scopes... May it be a bug in Python?
My patch helps to workaround this issue, but is ugly..

issue_title: ext2int function: bus numbering requires integer values instead of float numbers
issue_body:
I found a issue in the "ext2int" function related to false indexing of the consecutive bus numbering in row 152 (see following code). The dictonary data type requires integer indices instead of float numbers that are used currently.
The issue appears with the latest numpy version "1.12.0" (it doesn't ignore anymore false indexing with float numbers).

Current implementation (error appears in row 152):
```
## apply consecutive bus numbering
           o["bus"]["i2e"] = ppc["bus"][:, BUS_I].copy()
           o["bus"]["e2i"] = zeros(max(o["bus"]["i2e"]) + 1)
```

Manual fix by changing the code in row 151 (it's not pretty, I know, but it works):
```
## apply consecutive bus numbering
            o["bus"]["i2e"] = ppc["bus"][:, BUS_I].copy().astype(int)
            o["bus"]["e2i"] = zeros(max(o["bus"]["i2e"]) + 1)
```

issue_title: GLib 2.46 is currently required, can we target 2.40.2?
issue_body:
Along the lines of #4 GLib is something very hard to upgrade and you are usually stuck with what is on the system. 2.46 is fairly recent (less than a year?):

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GLib#Releases
https://download.gnome.org/sources/glib/2.46/

Is there something we could look into to bringing the requiement down to what is available on common stable systems? Maybe the specific method calls in 2.46? 

https://developer.gnome.org/glib/2.46/api-index-2-46.html

Debian Stable is at 2.42:

https://www.debian.org/releases/stable/
https://www.debian.org/releases/jessie/
https://packages.debian.org/jessie/libglib2.0-0

Ubuntu LTS Releases doen't have it until Xenial/16.04, the previous stable is at 2.40.2:

https://wiki.ubuntu.com/Releases
https://launchpad.net/ubuntu/%2Bsource/glib2.0

12.04 LTS until April 2017 is at 2.32.4
https://launchpad.net/ubuntu/precise/+source/glib2.0
14.04 LTS until April 2019 is at 2.40.2
https://launchpad.net/ubuntu/trusty/+source/glib2.0

Redhat and Centos are probably pretty  far back as well.

issue_title: More convenient way to extract magnitude in specific units
issue_body:
As mentioned in https://github.com/hgrecco/pint/issues/214#issuecomment-69291571 it would be nice to have a function to get the magnitude in a given set of units. This would look something like,
```python
def into(self, unit):
    return (self / unit).to('').magnitude
```

Such that one could do something like the following,
```python
u = pint.UnitRegistry()
a = 5 * u.liters / u.second
print a.into(u.cm^3 / u.year)
```

The name is up for debate. `as` would be great but unfortunately it's a keyword. I have needed a function like this quite often so I'm rather partial to the short length of `into` but it's arguably a bit terse.

issue_title: Stop pypcap from capturing and writing network traffic to a file
issue_body:
I'm capturing network traffic with pypcap and writing it to a file with dpkt. Problem is I can't delete the file as I get an error saying it's still in use by another process. 

    seconds = 15
    starttime = datetime.datetime.now()

    pc = pcap.pcap()
    file = open('file.pcap', 'wb')
    pcapfile dpkt.pcap.Writer(file)

    for timestamp, packet in pc:
        currenttime = datetime.datetime.now()
        timedelta = currenttime - starttime
        if timedelta.seconds > seconds:
            break

        pcapfile.writepkt(packet,timestamp)

    file.close

    time.sleep(10)

    os.remove(file.pcap) = file in use by another process error


I'm currently overwriting the pcap file the next time I capture traffic. Doing this overwrites the file data most of the time, but occasionally it doesn't work and I end up with a large pcap file encompassing two sets of network capture data.

issue_title: Add a pyang option to ignore a specific error
issue_body:
The pyang --ignore-errors option ignores all errors, but sometimes it is safer to ignore a specific error.

This could be done by adding a new --ignore-error option that takes an error name parameter (as -W and -E do).

For example, if a Yang module contains patterns with invalid syntax it could be handled using:

    pyang --ignore-error PATTERN_ERROR mymodule.yang

Pull request to follow.

issue_title: EnsembleVoteClassifier don't predict if classes represented as strings
issue_body:
### Code to reproduce bug:

```python
import numpy as np
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from mlxtend.classifier import EnsembleVoteClassifier

data = np.array([0, 1, 2, 3, 0, 1, 2, 3])[:, np.newaxis]
labels = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'a', 'b', 'c', 'd']

test = np.array([0, 1])[:, np.newaxis]

rf = RandomForestClassifier()
rf.fit(data, labels)
print(rf.predict(test))  # output: ['a', 'b']

clf = EnsembleVoteClassifier(clfs=[rf, rf], refit=False)
clf.fit(data, labels)
print(clf.predict(test))  # <-- error
```

### Stack trace:
```python
Traceback (most recent call last):  
  File "/_mlxtend_bug/reproduce.py", line 16, in <module>
    print(clf.predict(test))  
  File "/venv/py3/lib/python3.4/site-packages/mlxtend/classifier/ensemble_vote.py", line 197, in predict
    arr=predictions)  
  File "/venv/py3/lib/python3.4/site-packages/numpy/lib/shape_base.py", line 132, in apply_along_axis
    res = asanyarray(func1d(inarr_view[ind0], *args, **kwargs))  
  File "/venv/py3/lib/python3.4/site-packages/mlxtend/classifier/ensemble_vote.py", line 195, in <lambda>
    weights=self.weights)),  
TypeError: Cannot cast array data from dtype('<U1') to dtype('int64') according to the rule 'safe'
```

### Problem code:
```python
else:  # 'hard' voting
            predictions = self._predict(X)

            maj = np.apply_along_axis(lambda x:
                                      np.argmax(np.bincount(x,
                                                weights=self.weights)),
                                      axis=1,
                                      arr=predictions)
```

### Probable solution:
It happens, because in this case aggregated classifiers return strings, and predictions comprise strings (or chars), **not** numbers. So we have to transform them back to **int** labels to use np.bincount:
```python
else:  # 'hard' voting
            predictions = self._predict(X)

            maj = np.apply_along_axis(lambda x:
                                      np.argmax(np.bincount(self.le_.transform(x),
                                                weights=self.weights)),
                                      axis=1,
                                      arr=predictions)
```

issue_title: Consider mentioning when intersection_helpers._newton_refine can't be fixed
issue_body:
At the [end][1] of the [`intersection_helpers._newton_refine`][2] docstring, I mention a way to fix the method for tangent intersections (which force the Jacobian to be singular).

However, if the curves also have the same curvature at the intersection (which compares to a triple root in the same way being tangent compares to a double root) then our `DG` becomes rank 1 when we need it to be rank 2:

```
   B1(s) = [(2s - 1)(2s + 1)/8]
           [     (2s - 1)^2/16]
   B2(t) = [(2t - 1)(2t + 1)/4]
           [      (2t - 1)^2/4]
 G(s, t) = [          (4s^2 - 8t^2 + 1)/8]
           [(2s - 4t + 1)(2s + 4t - 3)/16]
           [             (2st - 2s + t)/2]
DG(s, t) = [         s,        -2t]
           [(2s - 1)/4,    -2t + 1]
           [     t - 1, (2s + 1)/2]
---------------------------
G(1/2, 1/2) = [0]
              [0]
              [0]
DG(1/2, 1/2) = [ 1/2, -1]
               [   0,  0]
               [-1/2,  1]
```

[1]: https://github.com/dhermes/bezier/blob/afea84e0bc70d5a750b2ab51e6eea951f677b5c5/src/bezier/_intersection_helpers.py#L279
[2]: http://bezier.readthedocs.io/en/0.7.0/algorithm-helpers.html#bezier._intersection_helpers._newton_refine

issue_title: Clarification on using db.all() len(db) etc. - Documentation

issue_title: nodeenv if not allowed to download iojs
issue_body:
Hi,

When I try to install iojs vie nodeenv, I get this stack trace:

```
 * Install iojs (1.8.1Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "ann/admin/venv/bin/nodeenv", line 9, in <module>
    load_entry_point('nodeenv==0.13.3', 'console_scripts', 'nodeenv')()

    create_environment(env_dir, opt)
  File "ann/admin/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/nodeenv.py", line 724, in create_environment
    install_node(env_dir, src_dir, opt)
  File "ann/admin/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/nodeenv.py", line 593, in install_node
    download_node(node_url, src_dir, env_dir, opt)
  File "ann/admin/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/nodeenv.py", line 489, in download_node
    tar_contents = io.BytesIO(urlopen(node_url).read())
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/urllib2.py", line 154, in urlopen
    return opener.open(url, data, timeout)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/urllib2.py", line 437, in open
    response = meth(req, response)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/urllib2.py", line 550, in http_response
    'http', request, response, code, msg, hdrs)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/urllib2.py", line 475, in error
    return self._call_chain(*args)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/urllib2.py", line 409, in _call_chain
    result = func(*args)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/urllib2.py", line 558, in http_error_default
    raise HTTPError(req.get_full_url(), code, msg, hdrs, fp)
urllib2.HTTPError: HTTP Error 403: Forbidden
```

Analyzing the network traffic, I see that we are trying to do an HTTP request for  http://iojs.org/dist/v1.8.1/iojs-v1.8.1-linux-x64.tar.gz, and we get a 403.

Looking at the body of the 403 message, I can extract this information:
 * Access denied | iojs.org used CloudFlare to restrict access
 * The owner of this website (iojs.org) has banned your access based on your browser's signature (21d0f4c3229b0aa2-ua48).

The way I interpret this, it looks like (until the iojs.org guys change their settings) it will be impossible to install iojs using nodeenv. (Or whichever library, that was responsible for generating this "browser signature".) 

However, downloading iojs with (for example) wget works just fine....

Do you have any idea where this "browser signature" thing comes from? Or why the iojs guys decided to forbid downloads from nodeenv?

Thank you for the help!

issue_title: Process cmdline is truncated on Solaris
issue_body:
`p.cmdline()` is truncated to 80 characters on Solaris 11. `/proc/PID/psinfo` that psutil uses to read the command line contains only the beginning - See e.g. https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/3934/how-can-i-get-a-full-process-list-in-solaris-without-truncated-lines.
The solution would be to use the same method as `pargs` tool - https://github.com/illumos/illumos-gate/blob/09f79f7c66b85f056db11f58210dc6182c9b1aef/usr/src/cmd/ptools/pargs/pargs.c. It's not however clear to me how can it be easily ported as it's using `libproc`, which is documented in https://github.com/illumos/illumos-gate/blob/09f79f7c66b85f056db11f58210dc6182c9b1aef/usr/src/lib/libproc/common/libproc.h as:

```
 * In the meantime, be aware that any program linked with libproc in this
 * release of Solaris is almost guaranteed to break in the next release.
 *
 * In short, do not use this header file or libproc for any purpose.
```

As a workaround `cmdline()` could try to launch `pargs` if available but it bears a `fork` cost.

Didn't test any other Solaris version.

issue_title: bug in Path.Arc._parameterize
issue_body:
Arc((725.307482225571-915.5548199281527j), (202.79421639137703+148.77294617167183j), 225.6910319606926, 1, 1, (-624.6375539637027+896.5483089399895j))

raises a ValueError
From http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG/implnote.html#ArcImplementationNotes
looks like p/n is the inner product of two unit vectors, and thus will always be between -1 and 1.

using 
(p/n + 1) % 2 - 1
in the two places you take an inverse cosine will fix this.

issue_title: I Am Router To Network Helper Function
issue_body:
Create a NSAP function:

    i_am_router_to_network(self, adapter=None, destination=None, network=None)

If the adapter is not None, the destination must be a local station address, local broadcast address, a remote station address with a network number that matches the adapter, or a remote broadcast address that matches. If the adapter is None, the destination must be a remote station address or a remote broadcast address and the adapter will be select by the network number in the address, the downstream NPDU will have a magical local station address or local broadcast address. If both the adapter and destination are None, all of the adapters will get a local broadcast message with the created network list. If the network is not None, the message will be constructed as a list with one element. If the network is None, the list will be constructed as if as unconstrained WhoIsRouterToNetwork was received on the adapter.

Add a deferred call to this after setting up the stack on router samples.

issue_title: Inconsistent Api.__auth checks
issue_body:
The twitter.Api's methods don't reliably check if the Api is authorized (i.e., `Api.__auth is not None`). (20 methods do, 33 don't.) Since the Api checks to make sure that you can't instantiate it with a `consumer_key` but not access tokens/secrets:

        if consumer_key is not None and (access_token_key is None or
                                         access_token_secret is None):

a more consistent way to ensure that you're not trying to make unauthorized calls, would be to move the check:

        if not self.__auth:
            raise TwitterError({'message': "API must be authenticated."})

to `_RequestUrl()`, since all calls require authorization at this point. This would have the benefit of removing some duplicate code and increasing the reliability of the check.

issue_title: Multiple instruments created when events appear before ProgramChange event
issue_body:
For example, if a `midi.Track` looks like this at the beginning:
```Python
midi.Track(
  [midi.PortEvent(tick=0, data=[0]),
   midi.TrackNameEvent(tick=0, text='Lead L', data=[76, 101, 97, 100, 32, 76]),
   midi.ControlChangeEvent(tick=0, channel=0, data=[0, 0]),
   midi.ControlChangeEvent(tick=0, channel=0, data=[32, 0]),
   midi.ProgramChangeEvent(tick=0, channel=0, data=[26]),
   midi.ControlChangeEvent(tick=0, channel=0, data=[7, 96]),
   midi.ControlChangeEvent(tick=0, channel=0, data=[10, 40]),
   midi.NoteOnEvent(tick=15996, channel=0, data=[62, 94]),
   midi.NoteOnEvent(tick=116, channel=0, data=[62, 0]),
   midi.NoteOnEvent(tick=24, channel=0, data=[71, 104])])
```
then two instruments will be created because `ControlChangeEvent`s appear before the `ProgramChangeEvent`.  This is arguably wrong; we arguably want to store non-note events until we encounter a note event and then add the remembered non-note events to the beginning of the instrument.  Handling this may take some legwork.

`        self.heartbeatInterval = heartbeatInterval`

`            while not stop_event.wait(self.heartbeatInterval):`

issue_title: Why we have `pbr` in `tests_require` in `setup.py`
issue_body:
@jorgebastida
 
on line 19 of `setup.py` https://github.com/jorgebastida/awslogs/blob/master/setup.py#L19 there is:

    if sys.version_info < (3, 3):
        tests_require.append('pbr>=0.11,<1.7.0')
        tests_require.append('mock>=1.0.0')

I wonder
- why we have it there. I did not find any use of it even in tests
- do we have any experience with `pbr` version 1.8.0 not being usable?

Originally I thought, this piece of code will become obsolete by rewriting the `setup.py` to use `pbr`, see issue #38, but working on it, I hit strange problems. One of findings is, that `pbr` has version `1.8.0` but even `pypi` reports the latest version being `0.11.0`. On launchpad I have found some notes about problems with version `1.8.0` due to conflicts with Sphinx and planned to be blacklisted. See https://bugs.launchpad.net/pbr/+bug/1496882

Currently I have setup.py rewritten to use `pbr`, and `tox` testing in place, but when run, it rejects installing `awslogs` dependencies. Any insight into this `pbr` related problem is welcome.

issue_title: Remove snkmail.com
issue_body:
Please remove snkmail.com.  Although it started out as a disposable email service (sneakemail.com), snkmail.com is a paid-subscription service for generating long-lived unique email addresses for different purposes. 

Emails to the generated address is forwarded to the subscriber with the email tagged so that he knows which email it was sent to, and with the From address altered so that replies go back via snkmail servers and to the original sender.  So this is really a service for identifying spam from websites, and not for generating disposable emails.

issue_title: Support for session_id in logic adapters so message contexts can be maintained
issue_body:
As I see, in `get_response()` we have this line:

`statement, response = self.generate_response(input_statement, session_id)`

and `generate_response()` does the following:

```
self.storage.generate_base_query(self, session_id)

# Select a response to the input statement
response = self.logic.process(input_statement)

return input_statement, response
```

as I see, the logic adapters aren't able to work with sessions. That is, they can't consult previous messages to learn the **context** of the chat (although the bot learns answers from previous ones). 

Wouldn't it be nice to pass `session_id` for logic adapters so they could maintain a context for the message?  This way we could implement **stories**, just like [wit.ai](https://wit.ai), not just static replies based on what the bot learned somewhere.

issue_title: Cannot delete files that have %XX patterns in their names
issue_body:
```
$ ia --version
1.7.6
$ uname
Linux
$ python -V
Python 2.7.13
```

When a filename contains a URL-style hex escape such as `%3a`, you can upload it but you cannot delete it:
```
$ touch helloworld.txt hello%3aworld.txt hello%3dworld.txt
$ ia upload test_filenames_percent -n -m collection:test_collection helloworld.txt hello%3aworld.txt hello%3dworld.txt
test_filenames_percent:
 uploading helloworld.txt
 uploading hello%3aworld.txt
 uploading hello%3dworld.txt
$ ia list test_filenames_percent
hello%3aworld.txt
hello%3dworld.txt
helloworld.txt
test_filenames_percent_archive.torrent
test_filenames_percent_files.xml
test_filenames_percent_meta.sqlite
test_filenames_percent_meta.xml
$ ia delete test_filenames_percent helloworld.txt hello%3aworld.txt hello%3dworld.txt
Deleting files from test_filenames_percent
 deleting: hello%3aworld.txt and all derivative files.
 deleting: hello%3dworld.txt and all derivative files.
 deleting: helloworld.txt and all derivative files.
$ ia list test_filenames_percent
hello%3aworld.txt
hello%3dworld.txt
history/files/helloworld.txt.~1~
test_filenames_percent_archive.torrent
test_filenames_percent_files.xml
test_filenames_percent_meta.sqlite
test_filenames_percent_meta.xml
```
The first `ia list` lists all three uploaded files. After trying to delete them, the second `ia list` shows that only `helloworld.txt` has been deleted (moved to the history subdirectory).

I suspect that the problem may be caused by an extra layer of unescaping somewhere, either by `ia` or by archive.org. When I look at the catalog entries for the delete operations, I see that the escape sequences have been interpreted; i.e., `%3a` has become `:` and `%3d` has become `=`:
```
[delete_single_file] => helloworld.txt
[delete_single_file] => hello:world.txt
[delete_single_file] => hello=world.txt

issue_title: Item.upload_file() fails for zero-length file with XML parse error
issue_body:
Using 1.5.0. This is a corner case, but `Item.upload_file()` fails if file is empty (length==0), with `ExpatError`: ``` File "/home/wmdata/deltacdx/cdx-shards-in-items6.py", line 417, in upload_shard item.upload_file(s.local_path, remote_name=remote_name) File "/home/wmdata/deltacdx/cdx-shards-in-items6.py", line 267, in upload_file 'collection': 'waybackcdx' File "/opt/wb/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/internetarchive-1.5.0-py2.7.egg/internetarchive/item.py", line 658, in upload_file msg = get_s3_xml_text(exc.response.content) File "/opt/wb/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/internetarchive-1.5.0-py2.7.egg/internetarchive/utils.py", line 172, in get_s3_xml_text p = parseString(xml_str) File "/usr/lib/python2.7/xml/dom/minidom.py", line 1930, in parseString return expatbuilder.parseString(string) File "/usr/lib/python2.7/xml/dom/expatbuilder.py", line 940, in parseString uploading wbdelta-0201-1 delta-20170401180002/part-0201.cdx.gz return builder.parseString(string) File "/usr/lib/python2.7/xml/dom/expatbuilder.py", line 223, in parseString parser.Parse(string, True) ExpatError: syntax error: line 1, column 49 ``` This is actually a problem in `requests`. it's not sending `Content-Length` header when data uploaded is zero-length, and IAS3 returns `411 Length Required` response with HTML content: ``` 411 Length Required

Length Required

A request of the requested method PUT requires a valid Content-length.

Apache/2.4.7 (Ubuntu) Server at s3.us.archive.org Port 80
``` I think `Item.upload_file()` could catch `ExpatError` and print raw response content to make it easier for users to find out the root cause.

issue_title: 0.26 is broken on Windows (fails to initialize the random number generator)
issue_body:
When check-manifest 0.26 tries to run 'python setup.py sdist' in a subprocess, it gets this error on Python 2:
```
WindowsError: [Error -2146893795] Provider DLL failed to initialize correctly
```
and the equivalent error on Python 3:
```
Fatal Python error: Failed to initialize Windows random API (CryptoGen)
```

This is caused by executing the Python subprocess with an empty(ish) environment (commit 396fda84345942526e7cf518aed3d87192c23d56).  See http://bugs.python.org/issue20614, https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/24160 for details.

My Jenkins caught this.

issue_title: Make a faster DatabaseCache
issue_body:
Django's DatabaseCache is a bit slow:

1. On every set, it does cull check via `SELECT COUNT(*)`, which means a full tablescan
2. It uses longtext with base64-encoded data rather than blobs
3. It uses a couple queries per set/add, rather than the single one that MySQL can do with `INSERT... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE ...`

... so make a faster one!

issue_title: KV value is returned in bytes rather than string when using Python 3
issue_body:
`consul_api.kv.get()` is supposed to return:
```
                {
                    "CreateIndex": 100,
                    "ModifyIndex": 200,
                    "LockIndex": 200,
                    "Key": "foo",
                    "Flags": 0,
                    "Value": "bar",
                    "Session": "adf4238a-882b-9ddc-4a9d-5b6758e4159e"
                }
```

It is true for Python 2.
When using Python 3, there is a minor difference for `Value` field:
```
                    "Value": b'bar',
```
which is bytes, not a string.

Thus, it makes Ansible [consul_kv](http://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/consul_kv_module.html) module, for example, not working properly as it badly [compares](https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/devel/lib/ansible/modules/clustering/consul_kv.py#L201) string value coming from `consul_api.kv.get()`:
```
>>> b'abc' == 'abc'
False
```
Suggestion: to return value as a string to make it working for Python 3 the same way as for 2.
```
>>> b'abc'.decode() == 'abc'
True
```
I am sure noone is going to put `b'text'` as a value into Consul KV as it will be stored exactly that way which seems to be a garbage.

issue_title: IndexError when you get an empty buffer
issue_body:
With an SSH session active, reboot or disable networking on the target machine (to close the remote end of the SSH session). Then, try to use paramiko-expect to scan through the shell. I would expect a socket.timeout, but instead you get an IndexError.

This is because the buffer is empty (on retrieval from remote connection), then you try to scan through the current output for the given regex strings, then try to directly access a 'found_pattern' that isn't actually found. 

This could by solved by either converting the While loop to a While: Else (for when the buffer breaks out of the loop), or checking that there is actually a found pattern.

issue_title: Manual S/MIME encrypted mails
issue_body:
I'm trying to send manually encrypted S/MIME mails.

I can manually S/MIME encrypt data with OpenSSL as I can't seem to find an option for exchangelib to encrypt with S/MIME.

From what I've understood, the easiest way would be to add the appropriate mail headers and encrypt the message body manually for the recipient.

So I believe the headers I have to add are:

```
    MIME-Version: 1.0
    Content-Type: application/pkcs7-mime; name="smime.p7m"; smime-type=enveloped-data
    Content-Transfer-Encoding: base64
    Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="smime.p7m"
    Content-Description: S/MIME Encrypted Message
```

The actual mail body would the just be the S/MIME encrypted (base64 encoded) message including optional attachments (e.g. images).

issue_body:
When a text contains typographical quotation marks like chevrons (»«) or guillemets («»), sentence boundaries are not correctly recognised. E.g.

```
from textblob_de import TextBlobDE

text = '''
Die Kindersterblichkeit nahm um dreißig Prozent zu, die Abtreibungsquote stieg. Die Heiratsquote ging um achtzig Prozent zurück. Nachdenkliche Köpfe ließen sich vom falschen Pathos nicht blenden.
»Immer muss ich wieder daran denken, wie schön wir leben könnten, wenn wir jetzt beieinander und Friede im Lande wäre. … Unter dieser Stimmung und Sehnsucht bin ich oft bedrückt und da bin ich am liebsten allein. Die militärische Welt ist doch manchmal recht unschön. Wie froh bin ich da, dass ich Dich habe, die Du so viel anders bist als die meisten Menschen.« Das hatte Robert Scholl am 1. Februar 1916 an Lina Müller geschrieben, die bei ihren Eltern in Künzelsau Urlaub machte.
'''

blob = TextBlobDE(text)
print(len(blob.sentences))
for sentence in blob.sentences:
	print(sentence)

```
gives
```
7

Die Kindersterblichkeit nahm um dreißig Prozent zu, die Abtreibungsquote stieg.
Die Heiratsquote ging um achtzig Prozent zurück.
Nachdenkliche Köpfe ließen sich vom falschen Pathos nicht blenden.
»Immer muss ich wieder daran denken, wie schön wir leben könnten, wenn wir jetzt beieinander und Friede im Lande wäre.
… Unter dieser Stimmung und Sehnsucht bin ich oft bedrückt und da bin ich am liebsten allein.
Die militärische Welt ist doch manchmal recht unschön.
Wie froh bin ich da, dass ich Dich habe, die Du so viel anders bist als die meisten Menschen.« Das hatte Robert Scholl am 1. Februar 1916 an Lina Müller geschrieben, die bei ihren Eltern in Künzelsau Urlaub machte.
```
Sentence count is one less than it should be.

issue_title: Circular Dependencies
issue_body:
Hi - First of all thanks for working on graphql-pynamodb!!

I'm having an issue with circular dependencies. I have a People table with people in it, some people may have other People as account_managers, some People may have other People as Dependent. I tried different  ways such as:

```
class People(Model):
    class Meta:
        table_name = 'flask_pynamodb_example_people'
        host = "http://localhost:8000"

    id = UnicodeAttribute(hash_key=True)
    name = UnicodeAttribute()
    joined_at = UTCDateTimeAttribute(default=datetime.now)

class Dependent(People):
    class Meta:
        table_name = 'flask_pynamodb_example_people'
        host = "http://localhost:8000"

    account_managers = OneToMany(AccountManager)


class AccountManager(People):
    class Meta:
        table_name = 'flask_pynamodb_example_people'
        host = "http://localhost:8000"

    dependents = OneToMany(Dependent)
```


or:


```
class People(Model):
    class Meta:
        table_name = 'flask_pynamodb_example_people'
        host = "http://localhost:8000"

    id = UnicodeAttribute(hash_key=True)
    name = UnicodeAttribute()
    joined_at = UTCDateTimeAttribute(default=datetime.now)
    dependents = OneToMany(People)
    account_managers = OneToMany(People)
```

And neither work because of circular dependencies. Is this something that can be done, how? Thanks!

issue_title: Add support for custom XML-RPC URL
issue_body:
https://github.com/danielperna84/pyhomematic/blob/master/pyhomematic/_hm.py#L385 currently hard codes the XML-RPC URL to access Homematic on as http://IP:PORT. This is a feature request to also allow specification of a path (and provide the necessary configuration options).

Reason: A homematic heating group (HM-CC-VG-1) that is made up of multiple devices (e.g. HM-TC-IT-WM-W-EU and HM-CC-RT-DN) on the CCU2 is internally represented as a virtual device (serial number INT0000001 and up) _on a different XML-RPC port and path_, specifically `http://IP:9292/groups`.

Steps to reproduce:
1. On the CCU2 create a heating group/team, for example as INT0000001
2. Look at the device list on XML-RPC port 2001: 
`xmlrpc http://${IP}:2001/ listDevices`
3. Look at the device list on XML-RPC port 9292 and path `/groups`:
`xmlrpc http://${IP}:9292/groups listDevices`

Actual results:
The heating group is listed on 9292/groups, but not on 2001.

(To work with these groups, a user would then have to create a second pyhomematic instance for the second XML-RPC interface, similarly to how working with rf and wired (and IP) devices works.)

issue_title: Option to return survival function for left-censored data?
issue_body:
The current code calculates the survival function for a Kaplan-Meier fit of right-censored data, but instead returns the cumulative density function for left-censored data.  In my field of astronomy, where the left-censoring often involves upper limits not on timespans but on other quantities (for example the brightness of sources, which can only be detected if they are above some fixed brightness value), we are often still interested in an estimate of what I would call the cumulative distribution of brightness, which I think is equivalent to the survival function ("what fraction are still alive at this time?" becomes "what fraction are brighter than this value?").  

The terminology between fields is a little different, but is the cumulative density function just 1 - (survival function)? 

Would it be possible to give the user the option to return the survival function rather than the cumulative density function?  I can see [here](https://github.com/CamDavidsonPilon/lifelines/blob/master/lifelines/fitters/kaplan_meier_fitter.py#L47) where one could make the switch in the Kaplan-Meier code, but I'm not sure if there's some subtlety in calculating the estimators with different kinds of censorship that I'm unaware of.  

Thanks for the code, and for considering this.

issue_title: Reloading matplotlib overwrites custom settings
issue_body:
Hi it seems like reloading matplotlib using ```imp``` as you do on line 81 in ```scalebar.py``` causes some issues with custom settings.
For example:

```python
from matplotlib_scalebar.scalebar import ScaleBar
from matplotlib_scalebar.dimension import _Dimension
import matplotlib as mpl

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
mpl.rcParams.update({'font.family': 'serif'})
```
will lead to 
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/10776763/23097117/3538f186-f62c-11e6-8da6-f50ad1e0b746.png)

As you can see the fonts are sans-serif and not serif.
Doing the same without importing matplotlib_scalebar, will lead to the expected result:

```python
# from matplotlib_scalebar.scalebar import ScaleBar
# from matplotlib_scalebar.dimension import _Dimension
import matplotlib as mpl

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
mpl.rcParams.update({'font.family': 'serif'})
```
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/10776763/23097125/7556059c-f62c-11e6-869b-0d1c30012cc5.png)

By playing around with a local install of your package I could verify, that reloading matplotlib (line 81) causes this issue.
```python
imp.reload(sys.modules['matplotlib'])
```
Removing that line resolves the issue and does not seem to change anything else. (all test still work and all other things I tested, too)
Could you explain what the original intention of this line was?

Best regards,
Arne

(PS: awesome package otherwise! Saved me a lot of time!)

issue_title: yf.download start & end dates error
issue_body:
The download function just works when the start & end date parameters are static strings, the problem comes when you try to format a date value to string, the download function does not recognize the parameters and throws and error.

```
import fix_yahoo_finance as yf
import datetime as dt

actual_date = dt.date.today()
last_month_date = actual_date - dt.timedelta(days=30)

data = yf.download("SPY", start=actual_date.isoformat(), end=last_month_date.isoformat())
df = pd.DataFrame(data=data)
```

The solution to this is creating static strings, f.e.:
```
actual_date = "2017-09-22"
last_month_date = "2017-10-11"
```

This way you can't create dynamic date requests, bc there's a formar error. I've also tried using 

> actual_date = actual_date.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')

and it doesn't work neither.

Good luck, thank you!

issue_title: Feature request: regex-style capture groups.
issue_body:
If my (very limited) understanding of jsonpath and jsonpath-rw is correct -- BIG "if" there -- a `.find()` returns the entire path through the object, root to leaf, that satisfies the given expression. So with an "un-rooted" expression like`field1.field2` on a complicated object, the thing you get back is yet another complicated object. You know that `field1.field2` is in there somewhere, but you have no idea where. Finding, say, everything "below" `field1.field2` means doing guess-and-check on the returned path. This is repeated work.

regex engines solve a similar problem by capturing parts of matched string during their search. "Capture groups" (also sometimes called "match groups") refer to the parts of the string that should be kept if matched. There are various mechanisms for then referencing the match groups on success, including by name and by number.

Something similar to regex capture groups would be highly useful in jsonpath.

(Apologies in advance if I'm a moron and a capture capability is already present.)

issue_title: reference etiquette question
issue_body:
I have some projects they look like this in structure:

issue_title: mx.sym.argsort() cannot sort array with large tensor
issue_body:
For bugs or installation issues, please provide the following information.
The more information you provide, the more likely people will be able to help you.

## Environment info
Operating System: ubuntu 14.04
Compiler: GCC4.4.7
Package used (Python/R/Scala/Julia): python
MXNet version: 0.11.0
MXNet commit hash :  568b5a2d3e701768ff6f270238e5edccc2f35ff1

## Minimum reproducible example
if you are using your own code, please provide a short script that reproduces the error.

```python
import mxnet as mx
import numpy as np

value = mx.sym.Variable('data')
sorted_adj = mx.sym.argsort(value, axis = 2)

coord_data = np.random.rand(32, 2048, 2048)
coord_blob = mx.nd.array(coord_data, mx.gpu())
e = sorted_adj.bind(mx.gpu(), {'data':coord_blob})
y = e.forward()

result =  y[0].asnumpy()

vis = np.zeros(2048)
for i in range (2048):
    vis[int(result[4, 0, i])] = 1
cnt = 0
for i in range (2048):
    if vis[i] == 0:
        cnt += 1
print cnt
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. The provided code will give the `cnt!=0`, which should be 0 because the argsort should return all the integer from 0 to 2047.
2. If the `result[4, 0, i]` has been changed to `result[3, 0, i]` , the result will be correct again.

## What have you tried to solve it?

1.If the size of coord_data is (10000, 2048), and the axis in argsort changed to 1, then` result[8000, :]` will cover from 0 to 2047 as desired; but `vis[int(result[9000, i])]` will be incorrect again. (I guess it may because 8000<4*2048<9000)
2. By several tests, it seems that the argsort function can only deal with the first 2^24 element.

In this method, there is always a space at the beginning. 
Is it needed ?
If no, replacing result by a list and doing a join at the end would be better...

```python
def _unicode_save_string_concat(*args):
    """
    concats args with spaces between and returns utf-8 string, it does not
    matter if input was unicode or str
    """
    result = ''
    for value in args:
        if isinstance(value, unicode):
            value = value.encode('utf-8', 'replace')
        result = ' '.join((result, value))
    return result
```

issue_title: Changing settings results in missing migrations
issue_body:
I just upgraded to 2.0.0, and discovered that setting `DJANGOCMS_STYLE_CHOICES` and `DJANGOCMS_STYLE_TEMPLATES` results in changing the choices for [`class_name`](https://github.com/divio/djangocms-style/blob/master/djangocms_style/models.py#L85-L90) and [`template`](https://github.com/divio/djangocms-style/blob/master/djangocms_style/models.py#L67-L71), which necessitates new migrations.

I've pasted the relevant bits below. It looks any djangocms plugin that uses this pattern (-link, -picture, etc.) will display this behavior. I am not an expert on migrations, so it's not clear to me what problems it might cause, but it make me nervous about future conflicts, and having migrations outside of source control.

In my `settings.py`:

```python
DJANGOCMS_STYLE_CHOICES = [
    'm-section',
    'm-highlight',
    'm-recommended',
    'm-content-icon',
    'm-content-title',
    'm-content-body',
    'm-column',
]

DJANGOCMS_STYLE_TEMPLATES = [
    ('recommended', 'Recommended For You'),
]
```

Output of `python manage.py makemigrations`:

```
Migrations for 'djangocms_style':
  0008_auto_20161116_1358.py:
    - Alter field class_name on style
    - Alter field template on style
```

Contents of `venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/djangocms_style/migrations/0008_auto_20161116_1358.py`:

```python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from __future__ import unicode_literals

from django.db import migrations, models


class Migration(migrations.Migration):

    dependencies = [
        ('djangocms_style', '0007_style_template'),
    ]

    operations = [
        migrations.AlterField(
            model_name='style',
            name='class_name',
            field=models.CharField(default=b'm-section', max_length=255, verbose_name='Class name', blank=True, choices=[(b'm-section', b'm-section'), (b'm-highlight', b'm-highlight'), (b'm-recommended', b'm-recommended'), (b'm-content-icon', b'm-content-icon'), (b'm-content-title', b'm-content-title'), (b'm-content-body', b'm-content-body'), (b'm-column', b'm-column')]),
        ),
        migrations.AlterField(
            model_name='style',
            name='template',
            field=models.CharField(default='default', max_length=255, verbose_name='Template', choices=[('default', 'Default'), (b'recommended', b'Recommended For You')]),
        ),
    ]
```

issue_title: Worker processes can't use multiprocessing (indirectly prevents usage of scikit-learn and nltk)
issue_labels: [{'id': 405299542, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/celery/billiard/labels/Issue%20Type:%20Feature%20Request', 'name': 'Issue Type: Feature Request', 'color': 'f9d0c4', 'default': False}, {'id': 405299650, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/celery/billiard/labels/Not%20Funded', 'name': 'Not Funded', 'color': 'd93f0b', 'default': False}, {'id': 405300375, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/celery/billiard/labels/Severity:%20Critical', 'name': 'Severity: Critical', 'color': 'fbca04', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
This is a spin off issue from the Celery issue [#1709 Tasks are not allowed to start subprocesses](https://github.com/celery/celery/issues/1709). The Problem was [first mentioned by ostrokach ](https://github.com/celery/celery/issues/1709#issuecomment-122467424) who provided a stack trace that occures when he uses a scikit-learn classifier inside a worker. Since the problem seems to be cause by billiard and not by Celery itself I'm creating a new issue here.
### Stacktrace

```
[2015-07-17 21:23:51,299: ERROR/MainProcess] Task app.f[329d0da4-2e0e-4e1f-8148-d64f47750b1f] raised unexpected: AttributeError("'Worker' object has no attribute '_config'",)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/username/anaconda3/lib/python3.4/site-packages/celery/app/trace.py", line 240, in trace_task
    R = retval = fun(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/username/anaconda3/lib/python3.4/site-packages/celery/app/trace.py", line 438, in __protected_call__
    return self.run(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/username/working/playground/celery/app.py", line 11, in f
    clf = pickle.load(open('clf.pickle', 'rb'))
  File "/home/username/anaconda3/lib/python3.4/site-packages/sklearn/ensemble/__init__.py", line 6, in <module>
    from .base import BaseEnsemble
  File "/home/username/anaconda3/lib/python3.4/site-packages/sklearn/ensemble/base.py", line 13, in <module>
    from ..externals.joblib import cpu_count
  File "/home/username/anaconda3/lib/python3.4/site-packages/sklearn/externals/joblib/__init__.py", line 112, in <module>
    from .parallel import Parallel
  File "/home/username/anaconda3/lib/python3.4/site-packages/sklearn/externals/joblib/parallel.py", line 23, in <module>
    from ._multiprocessing_helpers import mp
  File "/home/username/anaconda3/lib/python3.4/site-packages/sklearn/externals/joblib/_multiprocessing_helpers.py", line 25, in <module>
    _sem = mp.Semaphore()
  File "/home/username/anaconda3/lib/python3.4/multiprocessing/context.py", line 81, in Semaphore
    return Semaphore(value, ctx=self.get_context())
  File "/home/username/anaconda3/lib/python3.4/multiprocessing/synchronize.py", line 127, in __init__
    SemLock.__init__(self, SEMAPHORE, value, SEM_VALUE_MAX, ctx=ctx)
  File "/home/username/anaconda3/lib/python3.4/multiprocessing/synchronize.py", line 59, in __init__
    kind, value, maxvalue, self._make_name(),
  File "/home/username/anaconda3/lib/python3.4/multiprocessing/synchronize.py", line 117, in _make_name
    return '%s-%s' % (process.current_process()._config['semprefix'],
AttributeError: 'Worker' object has no attribute '_config'
```
### To reproduce

Create an empty classifier and save it as a pickle:

```
import pickle
from sklearn.ensemble import GradientBoostingClassifier
clf = GradientBoostingClassifier()
pickle.dump(clf, open('clf.pickle', 'wb'))
```

Create a simple app (`app.py`):

```
import pickle
import sklearn
from celery import Celery

app = Celery(__name__, broker='amqp://localhost//')

@app.task
def f():
    print('hello')
    clf = pickle.load(open('clf.pickle', 'rb'))
    print(clf)
```

Start the celery worker:

```
celery -A app worker --loglevel=debug
```

Run the app:

```
python -c "from app import f; f.delay()"
```

---

I myself also ran into the same error while importing the functions from NLTK (a popular Natural Language Processing library). The problem **only** exists when the code in question is actually run as a worker. It always the problem that the value that is returned by `multiprocessing.current_process()` has no `_config` attribute.

The current workaround for this specific error is:

```
from celery.signals import worker_process_init

@worker_process_init.connect
def fix_multiprocessing(**kwargs):
    from multiprocessing import current_process
    try:
        current_process()._config
    except AttributeError:
        current_process()._config = {'semprefix': '/mp'}
```

However, I'm totally not sure what this might break elsewhere.

---

To me, it looks like the problem was introduced by c7eedbd0ee1498e76d4fa1affac5b1a275660ee7. Prior to that the [`Worker` class](https://github.com/celery/billiard/commit/c7eedbd0ee1498e76d4fa1affac5b1a275660ee7#diff-42cbdfeee4eeeb2688454e975c2d18ffL238) was derived from [`Process`](https://github.com/celery/billiard/blob/master/billiard/process.py#L77) which indeed hat an appropriate `_config` attribute.

issue_title: Include Name attribute in Instance.iam_instance_profile
issue_body:
[Instance.iam_instance_profile](https://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/ec2.html#EC2.Instance.iam_instance_profile) reports the profile ARN and ID, but not the name.

This is strange since you can create new instances and associate an IAM profile with them by name, and if you want to lookup an IAM profile directly you can actually _only_ [do it by name](https://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/iam.html#IAM.Role). (Related: #768)

Can we have `Instance.iam_instance_profile` include the profile name in the set of attributes?

issue_title: Support for role_arn
issue_body:
It came as a surprise to me that all the AWS SDK's don't support the following profile setting in the configuration file.
```
[profile useful-profile-name]
role_arn = arn:aws:iam::<ACCOUNT_NUMBER>:role/<some_role>
source_profile = default
```
Will this be supported in any of the SDK's in the future? It's only supported in the AWS CLI. I find this feature to be extremely useful. Since it's supported in the AWS CLI, it would be nice if all of the other SDK's supported this. I only posted here since I believe that the AWS CLI is written in `python`.

issue_title: SignatureDoesNotMatch when s3 metadata contains two consecutive spaces
issue_body:
# Description

Including two consecutive spaces in a metadata entry triggers an invalid signature error when uploading a file to s3.

# How to reproduce
The following code shows the issue. Notice that the `'Double  Space'` string contains two spaces between the words:
```
import boto3

session = boto3.session.Session(region_name='eu-central-1',
  aws_access_key_id='***redacted***',
  aws_secret_access_key='***redacted***')

s3 = session.resource('s3')
item = s3.Object('**redacted**', 'test.pdf')
item.upload_file('test.pdf', ExtraArgs={
        'Metadata': {'key': 'Double  Space'}
})
```
Running this throws:
```
boto3.exceptions.S3UploadFailedError: Failed to upload test.pdf to **redacted**/test.pdf: An error occurred (SignatureDoesNotMatch) when calling the PutObject operation: The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided. Check your key and signing method.
```
However, removing one of the spaces and re-running succeeds without errors.

# Test system
- OS X 10.11.6
- Python 2.7.12 (macports compiled)
- botocore 1.5.26

issue_title: Collecting types from mocks causes a crash
issue_body:
A user trying this in a test using mocks (in Python 2.7) reported crashes. She told me:

- pyannotate tries to get `__mro__` from `MagicMock` when it gets constructed.
- `MagicMock` has 2 base classes — `MagicMixin and Mock`
- `get_function_name_from_frame` calls `getattr(inst.__class__, '__mro__', None)`
- `Mock` has `__getattr__` method defined
- which tries to get `_mock_methods`:
```py
        if name in ('_mock_methods', '_mock_unsafe'):
            raise AttributeError(name)
        elif self._mock_methods is not None:
            if name not in self._mock_methods or name in _all_magics:
```
- but `_mock_methods` has not been set yet
- because it gets set later in
```py
class MagicMixin(object):
    def __init__(self, *args, **kw):
        self._mock_set_magics()  # make magic work for kwargs in init
        _safe_super(MagicMixin, self).__init__(*args, **kw)
        self._mock_set_magics()  # fix magic broken by upper level init
```

##### Steps to replicate

Provide the steps to replicate (which usually at least includes the commands and the Pipfile).

``` shell
$ mkdir test_project
$ cd test_project
$ pipenv install --dev flit
```

I also get an identical error if I add a line `flit = "*"`, `flit = "1.0"`, or `flit = ">=1.0"` to one of my existing `Pipenv` files, and then run `pipenv install --dev` in that project. I can include that file too if that would be helpful.

The error appears to be due to the presence of `Requires-Python: 3` metadata in *older* releases of flit. Recent releases all specify`Requires-Python: >=3`, but I'm unable to figure out how to make use of this to get pipenv to install flit. If this is correct, it's unlikely that an upstream change to flit could fix this issue.

Some investigation into the history of Python version specifiers indicates that there could be other Python packages that will also trigger this issue.

issue_title: setup.py sdist produces a tarball containing .pyc files, breaking stdeb
issue_body:
`.pyc` files are compiled Python bytecode binaries, are specific to the particular version of Python being installed, and really do not belong in a source package as there's no guarantee that the package will be compiled on the *same* version of Python.

issue_title: WindowsError installing a package with a non-ascii filename
issue_body: Originally reported in cherrypy/cherrypy#1467, `easy_install` will crash when attempting to build a bdist_egg for a package which contains a file whose filename has non-ascii characters. The issue occurs in `rmtree` even if the stdlib rmtree is used.

issue_title: not compatible with this system or executable
issue_body:
Running virtualenv 15.1.0, I get an error message attempting to create a simple virtualenv:

```
PS C:\Users\jaraco> py -m virtualenv --version
15.1.0
PS C:\Users\jaraco> py -m virtualenv new
Using base prefix 'C:\\Program Files\\Python36'
New python executable in C:\Users\jaraco\new\Scripts\python.exe
ERROR: The executable C:\Users\jaraco\new\Scripts\python.exe is not functioning
ERROR: It thinks sys.prefix is 'c:\\users\\jaraco' (should be 'c:\\users\\jaraco\\new')

Note: some Windows users have reported this error when they installed Python for "Only this user" or have multiple versions of Python installed. Copying the appropriate PythonXX.dll to the virtualenv Scripts/ directory may fix this problem.

On this mostly new system, I've installed Python 3.6.4 and 2.7.14 from the python.org website to the `C:\Program Files\PythonNN` directories.

issue_title: Requirements aren't wired to setup
issue_body: The requirements necessary to run Firenado aren't wired to the setup structure and that's the reason why pip isn't installing tornado and pyyaml.

issue_title: Package refactory
issue_body:
The firenado framework holds lots of code in some __init__.py files and some imports are causing circular dependency errors.

This issue was inherited from the iflux original design when I was keeping some Java/PHP mindset.

In order to get a more pythonic approach and get the packing structure more simple all code on __init__.py files that aren't initialization should be moved to other files.

On this work the core package will die and the sub modules will be moved one level up.

issue_title: Is there a way to dump literal scalar style?
issue_body:
Really cool library! I like yaml very much. Especially because it is possible to embed html using the literal scalar style like: ```yaml content: |

Some paragraph


Another paragrph

```` Is it possible to dump yaml in that style wtih js-yaml? I know there are already related issues but I could not really find the answer to my question there. Thanks.

issue_title: BibUpload: preserve 8564 tag ordering
issue_body:
Originally on 2013-10-17

Currently, the `merge_bibdocfile_into_marc()` algorithm, which is triggered by `synchronize_8564` for every record involving FFT tags or `8564_` tags causes tags to be re-sorted. Sometimes, this might lead `bibupload_revision_verifier` to think there is a conflict or an unrequested change.

Order of `8564_` should be preserved.

issue_title: oauthclient: validate email address on user sign-up
issue_body:
For oauth services where the email address is not provided upon first sign up (such as ORCID) we currently show a sign-up form with an input for the email address but that email address is not being validated.

The email address should be validated but ideally the flow of the user wanting, for example, to submit should not be blocked until he/she validates email address.

So the proposal would be to do something similar to what https://balsamiq.com/ is doing. Upon sign-up you can start using the app right away but the validation email states:

> To start your trial, you need to verify your email address. Please go to the link below and log in:

> https://inspirehep.mybalsamiq.com/confirm/ac62ef1a8ce02277c901b61d86690e7fb3d1e1fb

> If you don't confirm within the next 2 days, your site will be suspended. If you don't confirm within a month, the site will be deleted.

> Almost there!

So we could allow for some days to access without validation and after that, block the account.

issue_title: views: impossible to use invenio-records-rest as a library.
issue_labels: [{'id': 414894984, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/labels/Lane:%20fast', 'name': 'Lane: fast', 'color': 'ffa868', 'default': False}, {'id': 473692875, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/labels/Service:%20B2SHARE', 'name': 'Service: B2SHARE', 'color': '207de5', 'default': False}, {'id': 273817405, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/labels/Type:%20enhancement', 'name': 'Type: enhancement', 'color': 'ff5500', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
**Problem:**
In B2Share code we were using invenio-records-rest with a few different endpoints (POST creates a deposit instead of a Record). Because of that we were using invenio-records-rest as a library and we disabled the automatic creation of endpoints (`RECORDS_REST_ENDPOINTS={}` in config). We reused the View classes and replaced the only method which was blocking us, i.e `RecordsListResource.post`. This does not work anymore as The views code depend explicitly on the config variable `RECORDS_REST_ENDPOINTS` (see [ext.py](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/blob/f0b44bc8b64ea01c13dbd0bc5736df81c8f9752e/invenio_records_rest/ext.py#L82) and [views.py](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/blob/f0b44bc8b64ea01c13dbd0bc5736df81c8f9752e/invenio_records_rest/views.py#L451))

From what I understood at the time this was implemented in B2Share, using invenio-records-rest as a library was an accepted use-case so I mark this as a bug.

Blocks #99

issue_title: PersistentAdapterRegistry not compatible with pickle protocol 2
issue_body:
This was first discussed in unreleated comments in zopefoundation/Zope#188. Putting it here where it belongs:

There's this nice warning message on the pickle protocol page (https://docs.python.org/2/library/pickle.html):

> Note: For new-style classes, if getstate() returns a false value, the `__setstate__` method will not be called.

The code in https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.component/blob/master/src/zope/component/persistentregistry.py doesn't work with that for empty registries.

In the `__getstate__` it removes all its in-memory-only bits (everything in `_delegated`), but that means it returns an empty value for an empty registry. When unpickling things, `__init__` isn't called, so this code relies on the `self._createLookup()` to be called as part of `__setstate__` to put those in-memory objects back. Only this no longer happens thanks to the empty `__getstate__` return value. I think that PersistentAdapterRegistry needs to implement a `__new__` to populate its invariants, maybe a `__getnewargs__` as well.

issue_title: Unhandled ObjectDoesNotExist in `jwt_get_secret_key`
issue_body:
### Steps to reproduce:

* Generate JWT token
* Go to <https://jwt.io/> and change `id` inside the payload with non-existing one (according to your DB)
* Hit endpoint with JWT authorization. You'll receive:

`BaseUser matching query does not exist.`

Code can be seen here https://github.com/GetBlimp/django-rest-framework-jwt/blob/master/rest_framework_jwt/utils.py#L26

### Steps to fix

* Exception should be handled
* Every `.get` method should be wrapped in try/except catching `ObjectDoesNotExist`
* If you don't want to try accept, do `.filter(...).first()` and check if resource is not None

issue_title: Missing dependency: SPARLWrapper
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm working on the `pyaff4` used by the Rekall memory forensic framework, and `rdflib` is one of it's dependencies:
https://github.com/google/aff4/blob/master/pyaff4/setup.py#L59
~~~Python
    install_requires=[
        "aff4-snappy == 0.5",
        "rdflib == 4.2.1",
        "intervaltree == 2.1.0",
        "pyblake2 == 0.9.3",
        "expiringdict == 1.1.4"
    ],
~~~

However, once everything is installed, an error is raised while 
 trying to `import rdflib.plugins.stores.sparqlstore`:
https://github.com/google/rekall/blob/master/rekall-core/rekall/plugins/addrspaces/aff4.py#L408

**The error is that `SPARQLWrapper` is missing as a dependency.**

So there is an issue with `rdflib` right now, becaus Pypi also says that `SPARQLWrapper` is listed as a dependency, but it's not being installed.

$ (venv) pip install rdflib

Thanks !

issue_title: SPARQL: COUNT DISTINCT not working properly
issue_body:
The combination of COUNT with DISTINCT is not working propetry in the RDFLib implementation of SPARQL.
Consider the following code:

``` python
from rdflib import *

g = Graph()
g.parse(format="turtle", publicID="http://example.org/", data="""
@prefix : <> .

<#a>
  :knows <#b>, <#c> ;
  :age 42 .

<#b>
  :knows <#a>, <#c> ;
  :age 36 .

<#c>
  :knows <#b>, <#c> ;
  :age 20 .

""")


print "Query 1: people knowing someone younger"
results = g.query("""
PREFIX : <http://example.org/>

SELECT DISTINCT ?x {
  ?x :age ?ax ; :knows [ :age ?ay ].
  FILTER( ?ax > ?ay )
}
""")
for i in results:
    print str(i[0])


print "\nQuery 2: count people knowing someone younger"
results = g.query("""
PREFIX : <http://example.org/>

SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT ?x) as ?cx) {
  ?x :age ?ax ; :knows [ :age ?ay ].
  FILTER( ?ax > ?ay )
}
""")


for i in results:
    print str(i[0])
```

It produces the following output:

```
Query 1: people knowing someone younger
http://example.org/#a
http://example.org/#b

Query 2: count people knowing someone younger
3
```

while the result of Query 2 should obvioulsy be 2. It seems that the DISTINCT in Query 2 has no effect (indeed, <#a> matches the query twice, as she knows 2 people younger than herself).

issue_title: Icon Change Request: fa-paperclip
issue_body:
My request is for a vertical fa-paperclip with its open side on the bottom.

The existing fa-paperclip is rotated by default. Its open side is top left, which means if you were to actually use a paperclip in that position, you could only attach it to a sheet from the bottom right. Of course that's not how paperclips are normally used. And this hurts immediate recognisability.
It's a small thing, but you wouldn't display an envelope upside down, even though you may find it lying around like that in your drawer. 

While the icon can be brought it into a position that makes more sense via CSS (transform: rotate(225deg);) it moves completely out of line then. So you have to apply more CSS to bring it back in line, etc. It would be nice if it worked right away.

issue_title: Bug when passing integers as extra vars
issue_body:
## Description
tower-cli seems to send integers as string.
If the variable type check is set on integer on the tower side, it makes tower crash.

## Scenario to reproduce the bug:

Under tower, create a job template with a survey
The survey contains only 1 question, 
"which version you want to deploy"
The input has to be an integer.

Then launch a job using `tower-cli`
`tower-cli job launch --job-template 18`


```
Error: The Tower server claims it was sent a bad request.

POST https://tower.yproximite.com/api/v1/job_templates/18/launch/
Params: None
Data: {"extra_vars": "{\n\t\"no_interaction\": true\n}"}

Response: {"variables_needed_to_start": ["'yprox_pr' value missing"]}
```

Which is ok.

Then, we pass extra variable file: 
```yml 
---
yprox_pr: 1553
```

```
tower-cli job launch --job-template 18 --extra-vars extra_vars_1553.yml 

> Error: The Tower server sent back a server error. Please try again later.
```

If I go under tower, and disable the "integer" check (I use "text" instead of "integer). It fixes the issue.

issue_title: Stricter requirements.txt?
issue_body:
The recent release of flask 0.11.0 required Connexion changes, per #240.

Since your `requirements.txt` says [`flask>=0.10.1`](https://github.com/zalando/connexion/blob/1.0.88/requirements.txt), the result was that all versions of Connexion that do not include that fix are broken.

As it happens this isn't a big deal this time; upgrading has just been a matter of figuring out what has happened and moving to a more recent version of Connexion.  But if, for instance, Connexion had changed more in the meantime then it might easily have been awkward.

This project can't predict what breaking changes might be released by upstream projects, so it would be more prudent to make upgrade of dependencies be explicit and not, as this time, accidental.

Therefore suggest that requirements.txt should contain exact rather than minimal requirements.  

(Probably it's OK to allow variation in minor releases so long as we can trust upstream to have some adherence to semantic versioning so eg something like `flask>=0.10.1,<0.10.99` would be reasonable).

issue_title: Request validator passes www-multipart-data when schema is $ref'ed
issue_body:
### Description

Request validator passes a request with Content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded when spec only accepts JSON and schema is $ref'erenced.

Interestingly, when schema is not $ref'ed, validation fails.

### Expected behaviour

Validator should reject such request, it contains bad content-type.

### Actual behaviour

Validator passes execution to application code, mayhem ensues.

### Steps to reproduce

Minimal files to reproduce:

#### Swagger spec:

```
swagger: '2.0'

info:
  description: a
  version: "0"
  title: a
    
host: localhost

paths:
            
  /test:
    post:
      operationId: api.test
      consumes:
      - application/json
      parameters:
        - name: body
          description: Just a testing parameter in the body
          in: body
          required: true
          schema:
            type: object
            properties:
              body1:
                type: string
              body2:
                type: string
      responses:
        200:
          description: OK
          
  /test2:
    post:
      operationId: api.test2
      consumes:
      - application/json
      parameters:
        - name: $body
          description: Just a testing parameter in the body
          in: body
          required: true
          schema:
            $ref: '#/definitions/Test'
            
      responses:
        200:
          description: OK
      

definitions:

issue_title: Turning off Algolia for tests
issue_body:
How do we turn off `algoliasearch-django` for tests? The way signals are registered, and the registration of models to the engine in `apps.py` has been breaking our test suite--we need a simple way to turn Algolia indexing on and off within tests with just a simple setting.

Is there any way to do this? The only way I can see is the `with self.settings(...)` modification in the docs, which isn't simple but also doesn't seem to work when we tried it.  We either get items indexing, or we get error issues that a model is already registered when we try to just remove the `INSTALLED_APPS` entirely.

issue_title: consider using plaster for config files instead of pastedeploy
issue_body:
Related to #14, we've written a new library called plaster for abstracting the config format away from Pyramid so that people can write their own custom formats. This can be updated in a bw-compatible way by depending on plaster_pastedeploy. Plaster can also be used for other purposes like sharing a config file with your message queue that is written as a gearbox subcommand or standalone.

http://docs.pylonsproject.org/projects/plaster/en/latest/

Again, I just wanted to bring it to your attention and am interested in your thoughts on using it.

issue_title: Profiling for forward/backward performance of each node
issue_body: It would be really nice if DyNet had a profiling program that ran forward and backward over each of the operations and measured how long each call took. This would help us catch any operations that seem to be taking way longer than expected, or operations where the backward step is much slower than the forward step or vice-versa. I think if we write a nice templated function it should be quite easy to enumerate all of the nodes, so this might not be *too* much work, and it would be super-helpful in making DyNet more speedy.

issue_title: Reading Argentinian H5 files and saving in CfRadial
issue_body:
Hi everyone,

this is not an actual issue of PyART but an enhancement proposal separated in two parts:

I updated the sinarame_h5 reader to match the odim_h5 reader. It's mostly the same but there are some 'unique' features from the h5 files. I will soon submit a PR with this stuff.

The second thing is something @swnesbitt tried to pull in #480. Writing cfradial from the h5 files is something I found really useful and I know some people who can use this as well.

This particular issue I found was on the way the original bufr files are created:  the variables are in separated files, they have different gate bins, strategies applied, etc. It's a huge mess to work with this data as is. I know this is a _very_ specific case, but I came up with a script (adapting something Steve has done) that saves all the variables and their metadata in one cfradial file. Should this be added as another function in the `sinarame_h5` file? Or, as discussed in the PR, it will fit better as an example or in the wiki?

I'm polishing that script (because it's in spanish right now) and I'll upload it a gist so you have a better idea of what I'm talking about. In the meantime let me know what you think of this addition.

issue_title: [Logging] Move the code path of calling get_gae_labels() to emit()
issue_body: Related with #3359. To make it easier to set the labels in each request without explicitly calling `handler.get_gae_labels()` or calling `client.setup_logging()` inside the request which causes a lot overhead, it's better to move the code path of getting the gae labels to the `emit()` function in the logging handler. And change the `AppEngineHandler` to extend the `logging.StreamHandler` directly instead of extending the `CloudLoggingHandler`.

issue_title: Investigate support for deleting stacks that have been removed from configuration
issue_body:
Adding a stack to a config file, deploying it, then removing it will currently leave it "orphaned" if you forget to run `stacker destroy` beforehand. It can, of course, be manually deleted using the AWS Console, but it's easy to miss it when you have hundreds of stacks.

It would be nice to have some way to enforce that a config contains all the stacks that represent one service. It would pair nicely with adding Nested Stacks support, such that a config could be represented by the parent stack, with all contained stacks as its children. Updates would go through the parent stack and remove any stacks that are no longer present.

issue_title: Design new PEX entrypoints to expose PEXBuilder
issue_body:
A core portion of the work accomplished by the `gather_sources.py` and `resolve_requirements_task_base.py` files is collecting their inputs into PEXBuilders.

Currently we build two(ish) pexes: one for the sources, and one for the requirements... and in both cases this is accomplished by usage of the `PEXBuilder.add_*` methods (for `sources`, `resources`, `requirements`).

In order to create mergeable [0] pexes from sources and requirements using `@rule`s, we should expose an API similar to PEXBuilder directly as a CLI tool. As a strawman, this might look like:
```
pex-builder \
  --source=com/blah/foo.py \
  --resource=com/blah/blah.txt \
  --requirement='libbar=1.2.3' \
  "${dest_file}.pex"
```

[0] We also need to decide whether we merge the resulting pex files using `PEX_PATH` or something similar to the pex merging API from `ResolveRequirementsTaskBase.merged_pex`.

issue_title: PythonInterpreterCache is not robust to `--python-setup-interpeter-constraints` changes.
issue_body:
`PythonInterpreterCache` is a 2-phase object:
1. setup
2. select_interpreter_for_targets

The 1st phase ensures interpreters matching the global `PythonSetup.interpeter_constraints` have been setup once.

The second phase uses interpreters cached in the 1st phase.

Say we run step 1 with interpreter_constraints: `['CPython>=2.7,<3', 'CPython>=3.3']`, we might get cached interpreters for `CPython-2.7.12`, `CPython-2.7.13`, `CPython-2.7.14`, `CPython-3.3.6` and `CPython-3.6.3`.

Now say we widen the interpreter constrains in a future run to: `['CPython>=2.7,<3', 'CPython>=2.6,<3', 'CPython>=3.3']`; ie: we now allow `CPython-2.6.x`. Since the set of cached interpeters `{CPython-2.7.12, CPython-2.7.13, CPython-2.7.14, CPython-3.3.6 and CPython-3.6.3}` satisfies all 3 constraints (the `CPython-2.7.x` satisfy `'CPython>=2.6,<3'`), the `PythonInterpreterCache` will short-circuit setup and now any python target requiring `CPython-2.6.x` will fail to find an interpreter in step 2.

This is the actual issue that was hit in CI for #5068.

issue_title: Support jar publishing to maven central out of the box
issue_body:
Right now we have no publish targets useable by default except to a local m2 cache directory.

Add support for the maven central publishing workflow.  This work probably blocks on #664.

Under ivy the mechanics are straightforward - undoubtedly even more so using [aether](http://www.eclipse.org/aether/).
Example ivy setup that works:
```xml
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<ivysettings>
  ...
  <resolvers>
    <ibiblio name="maven-central.snapshots"
             m2compatible="true"
             usepoms="true"
             root="https://oss.sonatype.org/content/repositories/snapshots/"/>
  </resolvers>
  ...
</ivysettings>
```

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!--
Copyright 2014 Pants project contributors (see CONTRIBUTORS.md).
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (see LICENSE).
-->
<!-- generated by pants! -->
<ivy-module version="2.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="http://ant.apache.org/ivy/schemas/ivy.xsd" xmlns:m="http://ant.apache.org/ivy/maven">

  <info organisation="org.pantsbuild" module="hello-greet" revision="0.0.1-SNAPSHOT" status="release" publication="20141001175724"/>

    <configurations>
      <conf name="default"/>
      <conf name="sources"/>
    </configurations>

  <publications>
    <artifact conf="default" type="jar" ext="jar"/>
    <artifact conf="default" type="pom" ext="pom"/>
    <artifact conf="sources" type="source" m:classifier="sources" ext="jar"/>
  </publications>
</ivy-module>
```

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

java -jar ~/.pants.d/tools/jvm/ivy/bootstrap.jar \
  -settings "ivysettings.xml" \
  -ivy "ivy.xml" \
  -m2compatible \
  -publish "maven-central.snapshots" \
  -publishpattern "m2/[organisation]/[module]/[artifact]-[revision](-[classifier]).[ext]" \
  -revision "0.0.1-SNAPSHOT" \
  -overwrite \
  -host "oss.sonatype.org" \
  -realm "Sonatype Nexus Repository Manager" \
  -username "jsirois" \
  -passwd "[plain text password here]" \
  "$@"
```

As part of this work, close this sonatype issue https://issues.sonatype.org/browse/OSSRH-11542

issue_title: Add the ability to set environment variables before running a junit test target
issue_body:
We have a maven build that wants to set some environment variables before running a test:

```
  <build>
    <plugins>
      <plugin>
        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
        <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
        <configuration>
          <environmentVariables>
            <DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH>${basedir}/library/src/main/cpp/native</DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH>
            <LD_LIBRARY_PATH>${basedir}/library/src/main/cpp/native</LD_LIBRARY_PATH>
          </environmentVariables>
        </configuration>
      </plugin>
    </plugins>
  </build>
```

${basedir} is the maven project directory, we could just hard code that for this use case.  What I envision is a new parameter on a `junit_tests()` target named *environment* that takes a dict as an argument:

  ```
junit_tests(name='test',
  sources = rglobs('*Test.java'),
  dependencies = [
    ':lib'
  ],
  environment = {
    "DYLIB_LIBRARY_PATH" : "project/library/src/main/cpp/native",
    "LD_LIBRARY_PATH": "project/library/src/main/cpp/native",
  },
)
```

issue_title: S3CrudPolicy does not allow for specifying a bucket prefix
issue_body:
<!--

Before reporting a new issue, make sure we don't have any duplicates already open or closed by
searching the issues list. If there is a duplicate, re-open or add a comment to the
existing issue instead of creating a new one. If you are reporting a bug,

## GENERAL HELP QUESTIONS ##
Github Issues is for bug reports and feature requests. If you have general support
questions, the following locations are a good place:

- Post a question in StackOverflow  with "aws-sam" tag

-->

**Description:**

<!-- Briefly describe the problem you are facing -->
I would like to create a SAM application that writes to only a specific prefix within a bucket and thus only have CRUD access to that bucket prefix.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create a SAM application that writes to an S3 bucket (see below)
2. Try to pass the bucket/prefix to the S3CrudPolicy "BucketName" argument
3. Try to create a SAM application using that file and you will get an error with invalid argument being passed to BucketName. 

```
Policies:
        - S3CrudPolicy:
            BucketName: !Sub '${bucketName}/${bucketPrefix}'
```

**Observed result:**
SAM application cannot be created due to an invalid argument being passed for bucketname 

**Expected result:**

BucketName under the S3CrudPolicy should accept any valid s3 ARN (such as bucket/prefix) but instead it only operates in entire buckets.

issue_title: Streaming IO support in docker mode
issue_body:
Just talked with @cdeepakroy about this and wanted to dump the proposal here so people could comment on it before I actually get started.

As of right now, IO for docker mode is limited to the following:
- Files mapped into the container as file inputs
- Command line arguments as inputs
- stdout/stderr pipes from the container process as outputs

All of this is done synchronously due to the design of our execution model, i.e. the output must be completely gathered by the worker before it is sent on its way. I propose adding two new features:
1. The ability to stream task outputs as they happen rather than waiting for the entire process to complete before sending the first byte of output. This will involve codifying streaming types for IO in some way or another, and I'd only be targeting their usage in the docker mode at first, but they could be generalized to other modes later.
2. The ability to use named pipes instead of just stdout/stderr, since those might be overloaded for things like logging. This could even be used to support something like progress reporting by having a special progress pipe. Ostensibly, docker should support mapping FIFO named pipes into the container just like normal files.

Please provide any feedback on these features, as well as ideas on how to structure the API/spec for them.
